description:
# Offline-First Core Architecture

Implement comprehensive offline-first architecture with local data persistence, queued operations, and transparent sync status. The app remains fully functional without internet connectivity, automatically syncing when connection is restored.

## Rationale
Addresses critical user pain point: 'Lack of offline access to travel plans when internet is unavailable.' Also addresses competitor pain points: TripIt server downtime (pain-1-1), Wanderlog sync errors (pain-2-3), and general travel app offline functionality issues (gap-2). This is foundational for travelers in remote areas, flights, or regions with poor connectivity.

## User Stories
- As a solo traveler, I want to access my trip plans offline so that I can view my itinerary during flights or in remote areas without internet
- As a traveler, I want the app to automatically save my changes locally so that I don't lose data if my connection drops
- As a user, I want clear indicators of my sync status so that I know when my data is safe in the cloud

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] App remains fully functional without internet connection
- [ ] All data changes are queued and sync automatically when connection is restored
- [ ] Clear visual indicators show offline/online status and sync progress
- [ ] Local database persists all user data, trips, journals, and preferences
- [ ] Conflict resolution handles concurrent edits gracefully
- [ ] Users can read, create, edit, and delete all data types while offline

acceptanceCriteria: Trips table defined with all necessary fields
Journals table defined with foreign key to trips
Users table defined for caching user data
SyncQueue table defined for pending operations
SyncMetadata table defined for tracking sync state
subtasks.notes:
Successfully created comprehensive Drift database schema with 5 tables:

**Created Tables:**
1. **Trips table** - Complete with all trip fields plus sync tracking (isSynced, hasPendingChanges, version, isDeleted, lastSyncedAt)
2. **Journals table** - With foreign key to trips, includes content, mood, location, images, and sync fields
3. **Users table** - Caches user profile data locally with sync support
4. **SyncQueue table** - Tracks pending sync operations with priority, retry logic, and status
5. **SyncMetadata table** - Monitors sync state per entity type

**Key Features:**
- All data tables include sync-specific fields for offline-first operations
- Strategic indexes defined for common query patterns (user trips, trip journals, pending sync operations, etc.)
- Proper primary keys and foreign key relationships
- Comprehensive documentation for all fields
- Follows Drift ORM conventions with @DataClassName annotations

subtasks.title: Create Connectivity Service
subtasks.description: Implement service to monitor network connectivity changes
acceptanceCriteria: Debouncing to prevent rapid status changes
subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented ConnectivityService with comprehensive network monitoring:

**Implementation:**
- Created lib/features/offline/domain/services/connectivity_service.dart (303 lines)
- ConnectionType enum: wifi, cellular, none, other
- ConnectivityStatus data class with connection type, isConnected flag, and timestamp
- Factory constructors for creating status from ConnectivityResult
- Stream-based API with connectivityStream getter
- checkConnectivity() method for one-time checks
- Debouncing mechanism (default 300ms) to prevent rapid status changes
- Proper resource management with dispose() method

**Key Features:**
1. Stream-based monitoring: Automatically starts monitoring when stream is listened to
2. Initial connectivity check: Performs check on first subscription
3. Connection type differentiation: WiFi preferred over cellular, handles ethernet/VPN/bluetooth
4. Debouncing: Configurable debounce timer prevents flickering between states
5. Status caching: Last emitted status stored to avoid duplicate emissions
6. Error handling: Returns disconnected status on connectivity check errors

**Integration:**
- Registered as singleton in offline_module.dart
- Exported in offline_services.dart barrel
- Uses connectivity_plus plugin (already registered)
- Follows project patterns from NetworkMonitor

**Acceptance Criteria Met:**
✓ Stream-based connectivity status updates
✓ Initial connectivity check on startup
✓ Differentiates between WiFi and cellular
✓ Debouncing to prevent rapid status changes

subtasks.title: Implement Network Reachability Testing
subtasks.description: Add actual network testing beyond basic connectivity checks
acceptanceCriteria: Actual HTTP request to test API reachability
Cached result with TTL to avoid excessive tests
subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented NetworkReachabilityService with comprehensive API reachability testing:

**Implementation:**
- Created lib/core/network/network_reachability.dart (470 lines)
- NetworkReachabilityResult data class with reachable/unreachable factory constructors
- NetworkReachabilityService with actual HTTP requests to test API reachability

**Key Features:**
1. Actual HTTP Testing: Performs real HEAD/GET requests to verify server is reachable (not just connectivity check)
2. Configurable Endpoints: Accepts custom test endpoint path (default: '/health')
3. Timeout Handling: Configurable request timeout with proper DioException handling (connectionTimeout, sendTimeout, receiveTimeout)

6. Response Tracking: Records status code, response time, and error messages
7. Cache Management: Manual cache clearing, automatic expiration, validity checking

**Error Handling:**
- Connection timeout detection with timeout duration in error message
- Connection error handling for network issues
- Server error (5xx) detection
- 405 Method Not Allowed fallback to GET
- Generic exception catching with detailed error messages

**Integration:**
- Registered as singleton in offline_module.dart
- Uses AppConfig.apiBaseUrl for API endpoint
- Comprehensive test suite with 13 test cases covering all scenarios
- Follows project patterns from ConnectivityService

**Acceptance Criteria Met:**
✓ Actual HTTP request to test API reachability (HEAD/GET to /health endpoint)
✓ Configurable test endpoints (testEndpointPath parameter)
✓ Timeout handling (5 second default, all timeout types handled)
✓ Cached result with TTL (30 second default, auto-expiring)

**Files Created:**

subtasks.title: Create Sync Queue Service
subtasks.description: Implement service to manage sync queue lifecycle
subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented comprehensive SyncQueueService for managing sync queue lifecycle:

**Implementation:**
- Created lib/features/offline/domain/services/sync_queue_service.dart (635 lines)
- SyncQueueResult class for operation results
- High-level API for queue management
- Enqueue operations (single and batch)
- Process pending operations with callbacks

- Automatic cleanup of old operations
- Queue size monitoring via Stream

**Key Features:**

   - enqueueOperation() for single operations

   - Configurable max retries per operation

2. Persist Queue to Database:
   - All operations stored via SyncQueueRepository

3. Recover Queue on App Restart:

4. Retry Logic for Failed Operations:

   - Only retries operations past their backoff period
   - Configurable retry count per operation
   - Automatic retry state management

5. Batch Operation Support:
   - enqueueOperations() accepts list of operations
   - processPendingOperations() handles multiple ops

**Additional Features:**
- Queue size stream for reactive UI updates
- Queue statistics (pending, processing, completed, failed)
- Automatic cleanup of old completed/failed operations
- Configurable cleanup intervals and max ages
- Comprehensive error handling with debug logging

**Acceptance Criteria Met:**

subtasks.title: Implement Operation Interceptors
subtasks.description: Create interceptors that capture write operations and queue them
subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented comprehensive operation interceptors for offline-first architecture:

**OfflineInterceptor (536 lines):**
- Generic interceptor for any write operation
- Intercepts operations and checks connectivity status
- Executes immediately when online
- Queues for later sync when offline
- Updates local database immediately (optimistic)
- Returns InterceptorResult with execution status
- Supports batch operations
- Configurable via InterceptorConfig

**MutationInterceptor (668 lines):**
- Dio-specific interceptor for GraphQL mutations
- Integrates seamlessly with Dio request pipeline
- Parses mutation metadata from GraphQL requests
- Handles network errors by queuing failed mutations
- Returns optimistic responses for queued mutations
- MutationParserHelper for standard GraphQL parsing

**Key Features:**
1. Intercept create/update/delete operations: Supports all CRUD operations via SyncOperationType enum
2. Check connectivity before executing: Uses ConnectivityService to determine online/offline status
3. Queue operation if offline: Enqueues operations to SyncQueueService with proper metadata
4. Return optimistic response to UI: Provides immediate feedback with temporary IDs
5. Update local database immediately: Callback-based local database updates for optimistic UI

**Additional Components:**
- InterceptorConfig: Configurable behavior (enabled, queueWhenOffline, optimisticResponses, etc.)
- InterceptorResult: Result tracking (executedImmediately, queued, failed)
- MutationMetadata: Parsed mutation information (entityType, entityId, operation, data)
- MutationInterceptorResult: Mutation-specific result tracking
- MutationParserHelper: Utility for parsing standard GraphQL mutations

**Integration:**
- Exported in offline_sync.dart barrel
- Uses ConnectivityService and SyncQueueService
- Follows project patterns with comprehensive documentation
- Debug logging with emojis for traceability

**Acceptance Criteria Met:**
✓ Intercept create/update/delete operations
✓ Check connectivity status before executing
✓ Queue operation if offline
✓ Return optimistic response to UI
✓ Update local database immediately

**Infrastructure Layer (sync_manager_impl.dart - 582 lines):**
- SyncManagerImpl with full sync lifecycle management
- Four-phase sync process: Upload → Download → Conflict Resolution → Finalization
- Connectivity change listener with auto-sync triggers
- Concurrent sync prevention with _isSyncing flag
- Auto-sync pause/resume functionality
- WiFi-only sync option
- Configurable auto-sync cooldown (default: 30s)
- Real-time sync status updates via StreamController

**Key Features:**
✅ Singleton sync manager (registered in DI)
✅ Start/stop sync process with force option
✅ Listen to connectivity changes
✅ Trigger sync when connection restored (with cooldown)
✅ Prevent concurrent sync cycles
✅ Comprehensive status reporting (state, phase, progress, errors)

**Integration:**
- Registered in offline_module.dart as singleton
- Exported through offline_services.dart and offline_sync.dart barrel files
- Uses ConnectivityService for network monitoring
- Uses SyncQueueService for processing pending operations

**Note:** Upload and download phases are placeholder implementations that will be fully implemented in subtasks 5.2 and 5.3. The coordinator framework is complete and ready to integrate with those implementations.

subtasks.description: Create base repository class with offline-first logic

subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented comprehensive offline-aware base repository:

**Core Components:**
- RepositoryOperationResult<T>: Wraps operation results with sync status tracking
- OfflineRepositoryConfig: Configurable behavior (enableOfflineMode, queueWhenOffline, optimisticResponses, etc.)
- OfflineAwareRepository<Entity, Model, CreateModel, UpdateModel>: Generic abstract base class

**Key Features:**
1. **Read Operations** (getById, getAll):
   - Try local database first
   - Return cached data if available
   - Fetch from remote if online and local unavailable
   - Throw exception if offline and no local data

2. **Write Operations** (create, update, delete):
   - Write to local database immediately
   - Execute remote operation if online
   - Queue for sync if offline
   - Return optimistic response with sync status
   - Temporary ID generation for new entities

3. **Integration**:
   - Uses ConnectivityService for network status checks
   - Uses SyncQueueService for operation queuing
   - Follows existing repository patterns (ProfileRepositoryImpl, AuthRepositoryImpl)
   - Generic type parameters support all entity types
   - Comprehensive error handling with AppException hierarchy

4. **Abstract Methods for Subclasses**:
   - Entity type name for sync operations
   - Entity/Model conversion methods
   - Local database CRUD operations
   - Remote API execution methods

**Acceptance Criteria Met:**
✓ Abstract base repository with common sync logic
✓ Read from local database first
✓ Write to local database immediately

subtasks.title: Implement Optimistic UI Updates
subtasks.description: Add optimistic response handling for immediate UI feedback
subtasks.notes:
Successfully implemented comprehensive OptimisticUpdateHandler service for managing optimistic UI updates with rollback capability:

**Core Components:**
- OptimisticUpdateState: Tracks optimistic update with previous state for rollback
- RollbackResult: Result tracking for rollback operations (success/failure)
- OptimisticUpdateConfig: Configurable behavior (autoRollbackOnFailure, maxAgeMs, enableTracking)
- RollbackCallback: Callback function type for performing rollback operations
- OptimisticUpdateHandler: Main service class (673 lines)

**Key Features:**
1. Return optimistic response on mutation:
   - registerOptimisticUpdate() method tracks all optimistic updates
   - Stores previous state before the optimistic update
   - Integrates with repository operations for immediate UI feedback
   - Returns tracking state for later rollback or cleanup

2. Update UI immediately:
   - pendingUpdatesStream provides reactive stream of pending operations
   - getPendingUpdates() returns current map of all tracked operations
   - hasPendingUpdate() checks if entity has pending operations
   - getPendingUpdatesForEntity() gets all updates for specific entity
   - Enables UI components to show sync status indicators

3. Rollback on sync failure:
   - rollbackOperation() rolls back single failed operation
   - rollbackAllOperations() rolls back all pending operations
   - rollbackOperationsForEntity() rolls back all updates for an entity
   - RollbackCallback performs actual database restoration
   - Automatic cleanup after successful rollback

4. Track pending operations for rollback:
   - In-memory storage keyed by syncOperationId
   - Stores entity type, ID, operation, and previous state
   - Timestamp tracking for age-based cleanup
   - pendingCount for quick status checks
   - Comprehensive debug logging with emojis

**Additional Features:**
- Clear operations: clearOptimisticUpdate(), clearOptimisticUpdatesForEntity(), clearAllOptimisticUpdates()
- Cleanup old updates: cleanupOldUpdates() with configurable max age (default 7 days)
- Lifecycle management: dispose() for proper resource cleanup
- Configuration: Auto-rollback on failure, tracking enable/disable, max age settings

**Integration:**
- Exported in offline_services.dart barrel file
- Registered as singleton in offline_module.dart with default config
- Ready for integration with repositories to register/clear/rollback operations
- Provides stream for UI components to monitor pending operations

✓ Update UI immediately (pendingUpdatesStream and query methods)

   - requiresBatteryNotLow: true constraint
   - requiresStorageNotLow: true constraint
   - requiresCharging: false (sync while not charging)
   - requiresDeviceIdle: false (sync while in use)
   - Exponential backoff for retries

**Additional Features:**
- Enable/disable background sync without unregistering
- Cancel all tasks or just periodic sync
- Schedule immediate one-off sync tasks
- Comprehensive debug logging
- Proper resource disposal
- Follows project code patterns and style
